summary: Iteration 7 backend testing complete — 14/14 new iter7 tests pass + full regression (147/147 across all 5 test files: backend_test.py, test_iteration4.py, test_iteration5.py, test_iteration6.py, test_iteration7.py). Verified the entire admin impersonation flow: start, customer-mode actions, blocked admin actions, exit, fresh restored admin token, and audit_logs creation/filtering. Cookie juggling (access_token swap + admin_original_token preservation) is correct. All previous iter1-6 tests re-run cleanly with no regressions.

minor.issue: Order of checks: customer-not-found check fires before admin-self check (since admin role != 'customer' filter). Result: hitting /admin/customers/{admin_id}/impersonate returns 404 'Customer not found' instead of 400 'Cannot impersonate yourself'. Spec allows either, but if main agent prefers 400, swap the lookup to {'id': customer_id} first then validate role/self. Currently 404 — tests assert 404 to match observed behaviour.

Endpoint does not require any authentication. Anyone holding only an admin_original_token cookie (and no access_token) can mint a fresh 12h admin access_token. In practice the cookie is httpOnly+secure so injection requires CSRF or cookie-fixation; still, consider also requiring a valid current access_token whose payload.impersonated_by matches the saved admin_original_token's sub before restoring.

On success, returns a fresh admin token with the standard 12h max-age. If the original admin session was already 8h old, the user effectively gets a session-extension via impersonate→exit. Minor (could be considered a feature).
No pagination cursor (only limit, max 500). Fine for current scale; consider since=ISO and after_id for >500 entries.

Filter param is named 'type' which shadows Python's builtin in the endpoint signature. Not a runtime bug because FastAPI maps it from query, but a linter will complain; rename to 'log_type' or alias='type'.

If the admin already has a stale admin_original_token cookie from a previous impersonation, calling impersonate again will OVERWRITE it with the current (already-impersonation) access_token — potentially nesting impersonation chains is impossible. Currently safe in practice because exit clears the cookie, but worth a guard.

HTTP 200 vs body statusCode=201 on /v2/sendMessage scheduled, past-delay silent fall-through, sentMessages prefix-anchor, url= SSRF, country-code heuristic, dispatcher TTL — none re-tested here, still pending main agent action.

action_items: None blocking. Iter7 features fully working as specified.

Optional: consider 400 'Cannot impersonate yourself' instead of 404 when admin_id == customer_id — requires reordering the lookup (drop role='customer' filter for the self-check, or check self before lookup).

Optional: harden /auth/exit-impersonation by also requiring a valid current access_token whose impersonated_by claim matches the saved admin token, instead of trusting the admin_original_token cookie alone.
Optional: rename `type` query param on /admin/audit-logs (shadows builtin).
Carry-forward (still pending from iter4/iter5): see iteration_5.json and iteration_6.json action_items.
critical_code_review_comments: /app/backend/server.py impersonate (501-568): correctly issues a 4h customer JWT with impersonated_by/_email, copies admin's current access_token into admin_original_token, and writes audit_log with admin_id/email + customer_id/email + ip + user_agent. Good. Self-impersonation guard at line 513 is unreachable for admin's own id because the find_one filter at line 508 already excludes role!='customer'. Either remove the dead check or move the self-check above the find_one.

/app/backend/server.py exit-impersonation (571-619): endpoint has no auth dependency and trusts admin_original_token cookie alone to mint a fresh 12h admin token. Verifying type='access' and role='admin' on the decoded token mitigates most risk, but adding `Depends(current_user)` and asserting current_user.impersonated_by would tighten this further.

/app/backend/auth.py create_access_token (31-49): now accepts impersonated_by/_email and a custom hours param; impersonation token claims are included only when impersonated_by is truthy. get_current_user (75-93) lifts the two claims onto the user dict so /auth/me surfaces them. Clean implementation.

/app/backend/server.py audit_logs (622-632): admin-only, supports type filter and limit (≤500), excludes _id. Sorted by 'at' desc — works because timestamps are ISO-8601 UTC strings (lexicographically sortable). Fine.

Cookie hygiene: access_token uses 12h max-age normally and 4h during impersonation, admin_original_token uses 4h. delete_cookie on exit uses path='/' which matches the set path — correct. All cookies httponly+secure+samesite=none — appropriate for cross-origin SPA.
server.py is now ~1632 lines (up from ~1462). Split-into-routers refactor still overdue (flagged in iter4/5/6 reports). Iter7 added 3 endpoints + 1 model change without modularising.
test_credentials: admin@wa.9x.design / admin123 — verified working at end of run (NOT changed). Iter7 test users prefixed TEST_iter7_*.
seed_data_creation: One TEST_iter7_<rand>@wa9x.com customer is created per run via /admin/customers and deleted in fixture teardown. Two impersonation_start + two impersonation_end audit_logs entries are written per run (one full_flow + one audit_logs_admin_access). audit_logs collection is NOT cleaned (intentional — these are the unit under test and the spec calls them 'audit' = persistent).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iter7 backend = impersonation + audit logs. All green. Cookie behaviour verified end-to-end via requests.Session — admin_original_token preserves the original admin JWT, access_token swap works both directions, fresh admin token issued on exit. Tests do their own cleanup via fixture (delete customer; exit-impersonation in admin_session teardown). audit_logs collection grows by ~4 entries per run; safe to leave. For iter8: if the team adds a UI nested-impersonation guard or a 'force-exit any active impersonation' endpoint, add tests for double-impersonate (currently overwrites admin_original_token silently) and for exit when admin user has been deleted in the meantime (currently 401 'Original admin not found' — already correct). Use BASE_URL from REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL — make sure to export it before pytest (the module reads os.environ['REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL'] at import time and will fail collection otherwise; this is consistent with iter4-6 tests). Total runtime: ~125s (60s scheduler wait in iter4 dominates).
rca of the issue: No issues found in iter7 features. All 14 new tests pass on first run after exporting REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL. The admin-self-impersonation case was clarified during test design: server returns 404 (customer-not-found) due to role='customer' filter ordering, which the spec explicitly accepted ('404 ... or 400 ... depending on lookup order'). Test asserts 404 to match the implementation.
